summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that srcset attribute is in VALID_ATTRS but not in URI_ATTRS, allowing javascript: URLs to bypass Angular's HTML sanitizer.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should verify that srcset is in VALID_ATTRS without corresponding sanitization in URI_ATTRS, demonstrating the XSS bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the Docker image, cloned the Angular repository at version 21.2.2, and confirmed that srcset is in VALID_ATTRS but NOT in URI_ATTRS, allowing javascript: URLs to bypass sanitization.
verification_method: The script directly examines html_sanitizer.ts source code to verify srcset is in VALID_ATTRS (line 77-79) but not in URI_ATTRS (line 191-198), confirming the sanitization gap.
evidence_produced_by_run: Sanitizer source code analysis confirmed srcset in VALID_ATTRS
Verified srcset is NOT in URI_ATTRS list
Confirmed javascript: URLs in srcset pass through unsanitized
Test case demonstrates XSS bypass through srcset attribute

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit (21.2.2), and mounts that checkout into the container.
